summary: New Zealand's guidance comes through the New Zealand Information Security Manual, maintained by the National Cyber Security Centre within the GCSB. Section 2.4 of the manual advises government agencies to inventory their cryptographically protected assets and begin planning migration to post-quantum cryptographic standards, pointing to the NIST post-quantum work as the reference.
govActors: NCSC / GCSB | National Cyber Security Centre within the Government Communications Security Bureau, maintains the NZISM and its post-quantum preparation guidance
provenance: Lead institution + posture: NZISM Section 2.4 (NCSC/GCSB, v3.9, November 2025, ingested)
Inventory + migration planning advice: same Section 2.4 row
NIST process: Section 2.4 summary states it references NIST PQC work; treated as the standards reference, hence dominantProcess NIST - REVIEW (single guidance section, no formal FIPS adoption named)
No FIPS family / algorithm names, no hybrid stance, no timeline or target stated in the ingested document - all omitted per iron rule
Only one included document; the 2022 v3.5 announcement was flagged (404 on verification, included:false)
Model 2026-06-14 (NIST-bloc / taker, Medium): NZ guidance aligned to the NIST suite; Five Eyes posture.
